archetype.name: Founder Product Explainer
archetype.editorial_goal: Turn founder footage, screen recordings, and product proof into a clear launch-style explainer with problem, promise, demo, proof, and CTA.

reference.media_policy: Reference only. Do not commit downloaded video, audio, frames, subtitles, or screenshots.
reference.notes: Timing and cue text are lightweight observations for planning fixtures, not a complete transcript.

target.pacing: compressed but understandable, with product UI held long enough to follow the workflow

observed_scene_timing.primary_visual: surprising result or product outcome before context
observed_scene_timing.purpose: earn attention by showing the outcome first
establish why the product exists and who feels the pain

observed_scene_timing.primary_visual: founder plus product framing or simple UI proof
observed_scene_timing.purpose: state the product promise in plain terms

observed_scene_timing.primary_visual: screen recording with optional face-camera picture-in-picture
observed_scene_timing.purpose: show the core workflow end to end

observed_scene_timing.primary_visual: real-world object, result, or physical validation shot
observed_scene_timing.purpose: make the claim feel grounded outside the UI

observed_scene_timing.primary_visual: clean final product or site CTA
observed_scene_timing.purpose: tell the viewer the next action

structure_sections.label: Cold Open Result
structure_sections.intent: Show the surprising result before explaining the backstory.
structure_sections.visual_strategy: Start on outcome proof, then quickly reconnect to the founder spine.

structure_sections.label: Origin And User Problem
structure_sections.intent: Explain the motivation and user pain with enough context to trust the product.
structure_sections.visual_strategy: Use founder talking head as spine, with inserts only where they clarify the problem.

structure_sections.label: Product Positioning
structure_sections.intent: State the promise and category clearly.
structure_sections.visual_strategy: Show the product surface, brand/site, or simple before-to-after system view.

structure_sections.label: Live Product Walkthrough
structure_sections.intent: Demonstrate the core workflow without losing continuity.
structure_sections.visual_strategy: Use readable screen capture, visible cursor/action states, and optional facecam picture-in-picture.

structure_sections.label: Physical Or Result Proof
structure_sections.intent: Validate the product claim with something observable beyond talking.
structure_sections.visual_strategy: Use real object footage, result stills, customer proof, or output examples.

structure_sections.label: Clean CTA
structure_sections.intent: Close with a direct next step.
structure_sections.visual_strategy: Hold a readable site/product frame and avoid adding new conceptual load.

transcript_cue_snippets.snippet: This is the result I wanted before I knew what to build.
transcript_cue_snippets.proof_requirement: Open on the result or generated output, not the setup.

transcript_cue_snippets.snippet: I kept losing the story between customer calls, product notes, and screen recordings.
transcript_cue_snippets.proof_requirement: Use founder spine plus quick inserts for notes, calls, or raw recordings.

transcript_cue_snippets.snippet: So we built Kino to plan the edit from intent, not from a blank timeline.
transcript_cue_snippets.proof_requirement: Show product positioning surface or planning UI.

transcript_cue_snippets.snippet: I drop in the transcript, approve the beats, and the demo compiles into a render plan.
transcript_cue_snippets.proof_requirement: Show screen recording of transcript, beat approval, and manifest/render plan.

transcript_cue_snippets.snippet: Then the proof shots make the launch claim feel real.
transcript_cue_snippets.proof_requirement: Show physical or output proof tied to the launch claim.

transcript_cue_snippets.snippet: Start with a recipe, bring your footage, and ship the explainer.
transcript_cue_snippets.proof_requirement: Hold final CTA on readable product/site frame.

inputs.description: User-owned founder talking-head footage with word-aligned transcript.
Readable product walkthrough recordings showing workflow steps.
Optional face-camera recording for picture-in-picture during the walkthrough.
Physical product, output, result, customer, or other real-world validation shots.

planner_outputs: KINO-EDIT.json with narrative arc beats and selected proof assets
vertical cutdown plan derived from the same approved story beats
picture-in-picture layout plan for facecam plus screen capture
source receipt and proof map for every claim
expected_core_flow: validate transcript, source receipts, asset candidates, and beat candidates
render master and variants with caller-provided media that resolves placeholder URIs

quality_gates: No committed binary media or downloaded reference assets.
The problem, promise, walkthrough, proof, and CTA are all represented as separate sections.
Every major claim has visible UI proof, physical proof, or spoken-context fallback.
Screen recordings remain readable in 16:9 and have a declared vertical cutdown strategy.
Approved beat token ranges are sorted and non-overlapping after manifest compilation.
